guides.title: Instagram for Creators
guides.tagline: Build your visual brand and drive traffic to your business
tips: Write a bio that states WHAT you do, WHO you help, and includes a CTA (e.g. '👇 Book me' or '🔗 Shop my looks')
Post Reels for reach, Carousels for saves, Stories for daily connection — use all three
Aim for 3–5 posts/week minimum; consistency beats perfection
Use 5–10 hashtags per post (mix of niche #fashioncreator + broad #ootd + community #styleblogger)
Reply to every comment in the first hour — the algorithm rewards engagement speed
Use your link-in-bio for all external links; Instagram doesn't allow links in captions
Collab posts (tagging a brand as co-author) get shown to THEIR audience too — use this for brand deals
Check your Insights weekly: track profile visits, reach, and link-in-bio clicks

stage_tips.starter: Focus on bio optimization and consistent posting. Aim for 100 followers before anything else.
stage_tips.builder: Start using Collab posts and Story polls to boost engagement. Research brands in your niche.
stage_tips.brand: Switch to a Creator or Business account for advanced analytics. Add a rate card to your bio or link-in-bio.
stage_tips.investor_ready: Use Instagram as a portfolio link in your pitch deck and LinkedIn. Screenshot your best analytics for your media kit.

guides.title: TikTok for Creators
guides.tagline: The fastest way to grow a new audience from zero
tips: Hook viewers in the first 1–2 seconds — TikTok's algorithm measures completion rate above all else
Post 1–2 times per day when starting out; quantity matters more than production quality on TikTok
Use trending sounds to boost discoverability, but put your own spin on them to stand out
Niche hashtags (#fitnesscreator, #foodtok) outperform broad ones (#fyp) for finding your real audience
Duet and Stitch top creators in your niche — it puts you in front of their established audience
TikTok LIVE at 1000+ followers unlocks gifts and boosted discoverability
Pin your 3 best videos to your profile — these are your first impression for brand scouts
Reply to comments with a video — comment replies perform exceptionally well for reach

stage_tips.starter: Post daily for 30 days straight. Don't delete videos — TikTok can resurface old content anytime.
stage_tips.builder: Apply for TikTok Creator Marketplace at 10k followers for brand deal access.
stage_tips.brand: Use TikTok Analytics to identify your best-performing content categories for your media kit.
stage_tips.investor_ready: Screenshot TikTok analytics for your pitch deck — brands and investors love TikTok growth stories.

guides.title: Facebook for Creators
guides.tagline: Build community and reach older, higher-spending audiences
tips: Create a Facebook Page (not a personal profile) for your creator brand — it unlocks analytics and ad tools
Facebook Groups are goldmines: join 3–5 groups in your niche and contribute value before promoting yourself
Facebook Reels (cross-posted from Instagram) is currently heavily boosted by the algorithm
Live video on Facebook gets the highest organic reach of any content type on the platform
Facebook's audience skews 25–45 — ideal if your brand sells products or services to that demographic
Use Facebook Events to promote launches, lives, or workshops
Link your Instagram and Facebook pages so you can cross-post easily from the Meta Business Suite
Check Page Insights monthly — track reach, page likes, and post engagement rate

stage_tips.starter: Create your Page and cross-post your Instagram content. Low-effort way to build a second audience.
stage_tips.builder: Join 3 Facebook Groups in your niche and start contributing. Groups are great for brand collab leads.
stage_tips.brand: Set up Meta Business Suite to manage Instagram and Facebook in one place with unified analytics.
stage_tips.investor_ready: Use Facebook Page data in your media kit — it shows you can reach multiple demographics.

guides.title: LinkedIn for Creator-Entrepreneurs
guides.tagline: Get taken seriously by brands, investors, and press
tips: Position yourself as a founder, not just a creator: 'Founder & Content Creator at {{brand_name}}' not 'Influencer'
Write a LinkedIn article once a month sharing expertise from your niche — it builds authority fast
Long-form posts (300–600 words) about lessons learned or milestones get 3–5x more reach than short posts
Connect with brand partnership managers, talent agents, and journalists in your industry
Use the Featured section to link your media kit, best collaborations, or press mentions
Comment thoughtfully on posts from bigger creators/brands in your space — you get seen by their network
Add your creator metrics (follower counts, engagement rates) to your About section
LinkedIn DMs to brand marketing managers convert better than Instagram DMs for B2B partnerships

stage_tips.brand: Start building your LinkedIn presence now. Even with 0 connections, it signals professionalism to brands.
stage_tips.investor_ready: LinkedIn is non-negotiable at this stage. Investors, press, and serious brand partners will Google you.
